schema_doc: Three-judge Bedrock-only council for the v1.1 system-prompt sensitivity sweep. All three judges run on Bedrock (zero OpenRouter cost). No judge organisation appears in the sensitivity eval panel (Anthropic, Mistral, DeepSeek, Amazon). Jurisdiction coverage: Middle East/Israel (AI21), US (Writer), US (Meta). Judge selection was constrained by live Bedrock availability — Cohere Command R+ is Legacy/denied, all NVIDIA IDs are invalid on this account. AI21 Jamba and Writer Palmyra X5 were the only verified-working non-eval-panel judges; Meta Llama 3.1 70B fills the third slot (Meta is not in the sensitivity eval panel — Llama 3.3 70B appears only in the main sweep as a control model, not in the sensitivity panel).

aggregation.note: Threshold 0.51: 2-1 split (agreement=0.667) is NOT flagged; 3-way split (agreement=0.333) IS flagged.

notes.bedrock_only: All three judges on Bedrock. Zero OpenRouter cost.
notes.judge_selection_constraints: Cohere Command R+ (cohere.command-r-plus-v1:0) is marked Legacy and returns 404 on this account. All tested NVIDIA Bedrock IDs return 400 invalid. Writer Palmyra X5 (us.writer.palmyra-x5-v1:0) and Meta Llama 3.1 70B (us.meta.llama3-1-70b-instruct-v1:0) were verified working via live probe on 2026-05-13.
notes.meta_llama_non_overlap: Meta Llama 3.1 70B is used as a judge, not Meta Llama 3.3 70B. Meta Llama 3.3 70B appears as a control model in the main sweep (sweep_models.json) but NOT in the sensitivity eval panel (Anthropic, Mistral, DeepSeek, Amazon). Organisation-level non-overlap with the sensitivity eval panel is preserved.
notes.main_council_overlap: Only AI21 Jamba is shared with the main council (v1.1.json). Cohere and NVIDIA are replaced. The S1 calibration check should note this change and use AI21 as the single-judge overlap anchor.
notes.content_filtered_on_judge_input: If a Claude eval response triggers Bedrock content filtering when passed as judge input, LLMJudge raises JudgmentError. The sweep script records non_responsive for that judge slot and continues.
